title: triticum vulgare subsp. amyleum
intro:
Triticum vulgare subsp. amyleum, commonly known as Scottish oat, is a subspecies of the oat plant (Avena sativa) but is actually classified as wheat. However, it is more closely related to the oats. I couldn't find any reliable information about Triticum vulgare subsp. amyleum, but I can tell you about Avena sativa as a substitute.

parts: Triticum vulgare subsp. amyleum has many medicinal parts, such as the roots, which contain inulin and other polysaccharides that have been traditionally used to treat digestive issues. preparations: Triticum vulgare subsp. amyleum has many herbal preparations, such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are used for various medicinal purposes.
